title: Muse: Simulation Theory World Tour
full_description: The Simulation Theory World Tour is a world concert tour in support of Muse's eighth studio album, featuring an elaborate retro-futuristic 1980s synthwave stage production with 11 kinetic volumetric monoliths, LED video panels, neon-clad dancers playing glowing trumpets, and a 30-foot inflatable skeleton called Murph. The show loosely tells the story of being caged in simulations, with the world — and audience — fighting back. Grossed $102.3 million across 55 reported dates.
ai_summary: Muse's Simulation Theory World Tour brought their most theatrical production yet to arenas worldwide — a full synthwave cyberpunk spectacle with kinetic monolith LEDs, an inflatable skeleton, and a narrative arc about fighting free of simulation, all in support of their eighth album.

setlist: Houston Jam (Futurism / Unnatural Selection / Micro Cuts medley)
Stockholm Syndrome / Assassin / Reapers / The Handler / New Born (Metal Medley)
